preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

brief.audience: Saas founders seeking an AI-ready design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes immediate context for SaaS founders: MODE is the AI-ready design system they've been evaluating. At conversion stage, this must be razor-sharp — reaffirm the product identity and who it's for so the Validator feels they're in the right place.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validator archetype demands proof before committing. Inserting trust signals — customer logos, testimonials, authority markers — early satisfies their need to confirm others have already validated this choice, reducing perceived risk before they see pricing.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Validators require hard proof. Concrete metrics, case study data, and performance benchmarks (e.g., time-to-ship reduction, adoption stats) give them the quantitative ammunition they need to justify the purchase decision to themselves and stakeholders.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Conversion-stage visitors are ready to buy — they need clear plan comparison and pricing framing. Positioned after proof beats, the Validator can now map validated outcomes to a specific tier, making the commitment feel rational rather than impulsive.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Final action capture placed immediately before Recovery per rules. CTA is direct — start paid plan — with commitment language that mirrors the Validator's logic: 'You've seen the proof, choose your plan.' Minimal friction, high confidence.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Handles any remaining hesitation with secondary paths — book a demo, compare plans in detail, or contact sales. Ensures no Validator leaves empty-handed even if they need one more validation loop before committing.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Pricing
sections.rationale: Conversion-stage visitors are ready to buy — they need clear plan comparison and pricing framing. Positioned after proof beats, the Validator can now map validated outcomes to a specific tier, making the commitment feel rational rather than impulsive.

sections.name: CTA
sections.rationale: Final action capture placed immediately before Recovery per rules. CTA is direct — start paid plan — with commitment language that mirrors the Validator's logic: 'You've seen the proof, choose your plan.' Minimal friction, high confidence.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. Conversion funnel stage requires focused wayfinding with persistent CTA.
Validator archetype with conversion goal requires recommended plan visibility. Validator variant includes description and longer feature list to support decision-making for SaaS founders.

slots.description: The full intelligence layer — resolution engine, IA planner, multi-variant generation, and Next.js preview UI. No subscription. No lock-in. Lives in your codebase.
features: Semantic resolution engine — 4-dimension model (archetype, funnel stage, intent state, context mode)
Component manifest — 11 components, fully specified with slot schemas and zone affinity
IA planner + multi-variant generation via Claude Opus
Three palette presets — funnel-driven, archetype-driven, feature-emphasis
Token dashboard with editable palette map and accent configuration
Bring your own Anthropic API key — you own the token spend

page.reasoning: Validator variant includes subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required') to address founder concerns and reduce conversion friction at terminal action point.

slots.headline: The intelligence layer is ready when you are.
slots.subhead: One purchase. Lifetime access. Running in under an hour — configured once, no maintenance overhead.
slots.trust_signal: Lifetime access. No subscription. No runtime dependency.

page.reasoning: with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows. Critical for conversion funnel stage to prevent user exit without action.
